author: Jacob Janssonius
title: In Sacrum Missae Canonem quo Romana utitur Ecclesia Expositio
remarks: Long preface on the spiritual nature of sacrifice. W/ a short preface invoking S Bernard, it is a Commentary on the Offertory, Canon, and Communion. Remarks on each word or phrase are rich: doctrinal, ascetical, moral, patristic (esp. Irenaeus, Augustine, Ambrose, Cyril, Bede, Dionysius, Bernard) and scriptural. No division and no citation of the previous tradition, almost no historical remarks. Nothing about ceremonial (except at consecration) or signs of the cross. Little polemic, not much on transubstantiation. The consecration is a commemoration of his death but not allegorically. Pg 161: signs of the cross at Per ipsum
